text:
Thank you…?… Good evening audience. So happy to be here this closing evening of our services here in the--this lovely country that I love with all my heart--America. I think of our forefathers when they sing:
Long may our land be bright,
With freedom's holy light,
Protect us by Thy might,
Great God our King.
text_no:
Takk... God kveld, publikum. Det er en glede å være her på den avsluttende kvelden av møtene våre i dette vakre landet som jeg elsker av hele mitt hjerte – Amerika. Jeg tenker på våre forfedre når de synger:
Må vårt land lenge skinne klart,
Med frihetens hellige lys,
Beskytt oss ved Din kraft,
Store Gud, vår Konge.

text:
I'm so thankful to go to other nations to represent a nation like this, America, where we have freedom of religion, and we can speak as--as we feel led the Spirit of God to speak. And I'm happy that… to feel the call of the Gospel that Jesus Christ, the Son of God, has called me to go to other countries to--to minister to them.
I shall always in my journey remember this last night, remember New York, remember all you people. You've been so kind to us, this short stay we've had. I trust that someday you'll permit me to come back to--to be with you again, and… Thank you.
I've all my life, I've cherished friends. There's nothing I love like people, and I wanted people to love me.
text_no:
Jeg er så takknemlig for å kunne reise til andre nasjoner og representere et land som dette, Amerika, hvor vi har religionsfrihet og kan tale slik vi føler oss ledet av Guds Ånd. Jeg er lykkelig for å føle kallet til Evangeliet som Jesus Kristus, Guds Sønn, har gitt meg, nemlig å reise til andre land og forkynne for dem.
Jeg vil alltid huske denne siste kvelden på min reise, huske New York og alle dere mennesker. Dere har vært så vennlige mot oss i den korte tiden vi har vært her. Jeg håper at dere en dag vil la meg komme tilbake for å være sammen med dere igjen. Takk.
Hele mitt liv har jeg verdsatt venner. Det finnes ingenting jeg elsker mer enn mennesker, og jeg ønsket at mennesker skulle elske meg.

text:
In the younger days, I did not have very many friends. I was more or less… Well, I… As a boy not smoking, and dancing, and so forth, why, people thought I was a sissy, my friends. Then later on in my church when I was converted a few years ago, I seen the Gospel as the power of God, and I was a fanatic unto them. And then later when I found there was a group who believed me and accepted me as their brother, my, certainly thankful to--that God has placed me with you.
And now, I thank each one of you a few moments ago when you were singing, "For You I Am Praying." That's what I want you to do. It's--it's prayer that I depend on. That's the secret of all the mysteries of God. That's the key that opens the door to everything of God, is prayer. If you pray and believe, when you pray, then believe that you receive what you ask for, and God will make it known.
text_no:
I min ungdom hadde jeg ikke mange venner. Jeg var mer eller mindre… Vel, jeg... Som en gutt som ikke røykte og danset, og så videre, tenkte folk, inkludert mine venner, at jeg var pysete. Senere, da jeg ble omvendt i menigheten min for noen år siden, så jeg Evangeliet som Guds kraft, og for dem var jeg en fanatiker. Men etter hvert fant jeg en gruppe som trodde på meg og aksepterte meg som deres Bror. Jeg er svært takknemlig for at Gud har plassert meg blant dere.
Jeg vil også takke hver enkelt av dere for en stund siden, da dere sang "For You I Am Praying." Det er det jeg ønsker dere skal gjøre. Det er bønn jeg avhenger av. Bønn er nøkkelen som åpner døren til alle Guds mysterier. Hvis du ber og tror når du ber, tro da at du mottar det du ber om, og Gud vil gjøre det kjent.

text:
A minister called me awhile ago from down in Louisiana. They got all mixed up, and has left his church because of someone calling him out. He said, "Brother Branham, I just like to know." Said, "They told me I was a something another in the church." And said, "I've left my church, and I'm… Don't know what to do now."
I said, "I would advise you, go right back to your church again." God isn't calling people out of their churches, He's calling them to Him, to Himself, to unite us together.
text_no:
En forkynner fra Louisiana ringte meg for en tid siden. Han fortalte at det hadde oppstått forvirring, og at han hadde forlatt sin menighet fordi noen hadde kalt ham ut. Han sa, "Bror Branham, jeg vil bare vite én ting." Han fortsatte, "De sa til meg at jeg var noe i menigheten." Og så sa han, "Jeg har forlatt menigheten min, og jeg... vet ikke hva jeg skal gjøre nå."
Jeg svarte, "Jeg vil anbefale deg å dra rett tilbake til menigheten din. Gud kaller ikke folk ut av sine menigheter; Han kaller dem til Seg, for å forene oss."

text:
Know at one time the Full Gospel people were considered a bunch of fanatics. We lived in--in the alley, anywhere that we could get a place to preach. And… But now, the Samson's hair's growed out, and it's become united together. When they were broken up in different denominations and had a religious prejudice, well, God could not deal with the people. But now when that middle wall is broken down, and we've come together as one unit, it's the most mighty church on the face the earth.
And you know, most all famous pictures, before they're hung in a hall of fame, they have to go through the hall of critics first, and have to pass the critics. Then when it passes the critics, then it can be hung in a hall of fame.
Now, I think that's our little church. It's passed through every criticism, been called every black and ugly name. But I think God's going to hang it in the hall of fame one of these mornings and going to take it up, and I--I want to be with them when they go.
text_no:
En gang ble de som tilhørte Full Gospel ansett som en gjeng fanatikere. Vi holdt til i bakgater, hvor som helst vi kunne finne et sted å forkynne. Men nå har Samsons hår vokst ut, og vi har blitt forent. Da vi var oppdelt i forskjellige konfesjoner og hadde religiøse fordommer, kunne ikke Gud arbeide med oss. Men nå når denne skilleveggen er brutt ned, og vi har kommet sammen som én enhet, er det den mektigste menigheten på jordens overflate.
De fleste kjente malerier må gjennomgå kritikk før de blir hengt opp i et æreshall, og må bestå kritikken. Når de har bestått, kan de henges i æreshallen.
Jeg tror vår lille menighet har gått gjennom all mulig kritikk og blitt kalt alle slags stygge navn. Men jeg tror Gud en morgen vil henge den i æreshallen og ta den opp til Himmel, og jeg vil være med dem når de går.

text:
Don't forget to pray for me. And when you're… when you're praying, and whenever you think of me, sing, "Only Believe," will you. That's my favorite song. It's a…
Paul Rader wrote that song. Paul Rader was a friend of mine. And I just come into the church one night it was being played. I didn't even know that he wrote the song. And right immediately after the Angel of the Lord had visit me. And something just struck me. And ever since then, it's been the campaign theme song, "Only Believe."
If I shall go and have to be buried before Jesus comes, it's arranged that when I go down beneath the dirt, why, that they're going to sing "Only Believe, all things are possible." If you hear of me going that hour, will you just stop somewhere and remember me? Hum to yourself, "Only Believe." For I believe that someday that I shall come out again.
text_no:
Ikke glem å be for meg. Når du ber, og når du tenker på meg, syng "Only Believe" for meg. Det er favorittsangen min. 
Paul Rader skrev den sangen. Paul Rader var en venn av meg. En kveld kom jeg inn i menigheten mens den ble spilt. Jeg visste ikke engang at han hadde skrevet sangen. Rett etter at Herrens Engel hadde besøkt meg, rørte noe ved meg. Siden da har "Only Believe" vært temasangen for møtene.
Hvis jeg skulle gå bort og bli gravlagt før Jesus kommer, er det arrangert at de skal synge "Only Believe, all things are possible" når jeg legges i jorden. Hvis du hører om min bortgang, vil du da stoppe opp et øyeblikk og huske på meg? Nynn "Only Believe" for deg selv. For jeg tror at en dag skal jeg komme tilbake.

text:
We're going towards a dark chamber, a chamber of death. Everyone enters that chamber. And every time our heart beats, we go one step closer. Someday, I shall enter that chamber. And when I get down to the last heart beat, I do not want to go in there as a coward; I want to wrap myself in His righteousness; pull it up around me and enter that chamber, to know Him in the power of His resurrection. And when they… The Angel screams forth, the Voice, the trumpet sounds, I want to come out from among the dead.
I hope to see you all there. How I would like to take each one tonight, set down and talk to you for hours. That's almost impossible for me to do that. But I will make an appointment with you, and by grace of God, I'll keep it. If you'll make it with me, someday when it's over let's set down by the rivers of Life over there, where we'll just talk a thousand years with each one of us, just talk over the old things.
text_no:
Vi går mot et mørkt kammer, et dødens kammer. Alle trer inn i dette kammeret. Hver gang hjertet vårt slår, tar vi ett steg nærmere. En dag skal Jeg tre inn i det kammeret. Når Jeg når det siste hjerteslaget, vil Jeg ikke gå inn som en feiging; Jeg vil ikle Meg Hans rettferdighet, trekke den rundt Meg og gå inn i kammeret, og kjenne Ham i kraften av Hans oppstandelse. Og når Engelen roper ut, når Røsten og trompeten lyder, vil Jeg stå opp fra de døde.
Jeg håper å se dere alle der. Hvor gjerne Jeg skulle ønske å ta hver enkelt av dere i kveld, sette Meg ned og snakke med dere i timevis. Det er nesten umulig for Meg å gjøre det. Men Jeg skal gjøre en avtale med dere, og ved Guds nåde, skal Jeg holde den. Hvis dere inngår denne avtalen med Meg, kan vi en dag, når det hele er over, sette oss ved Livets elver der borte. Vi kan snakke i tusen år med hver og en, mimre om de gamle tingene.

text:
I believe it's going to be there. I believe it's going to be real, just as the Bible says it is. And I… We won't have no less time. Then when I get through talking to you all, we'll just have the same amount of time, and it'll be a glorious time. And we're trying hard, struggling to get to that place.
I've… Not to take up much of your time, but I… Just before leaving here, I would just like to say a few words. I was just thinking a few moments ago when I come up, some of my friends here from Arkansas, and different parts of the country, has come in to say good-bye to us tomorrow. And I'm certainly thankful for that.
I see a young lady over here at… piano. And was just in a meeting, and… where I was preaching, or trying to preach. I'm not a preacher, but I… She received the baptism of the Spirit in the--in the service. And so I was glad to see her.
And Brother Moore just told me there was several here from different parts of the country. And we're thankful for that. Hope to be back someday with you all again.
text_no:
Jeg tror det kommer til å skje. Jeg tror det kommer til å bli virkelig, akkurat som Bibelen sier. Og jeg... Vi får ikke mindre tid. Når jeg er ferdig med å snakke til dere, vil vi ha like mye tid igjen, og det vil bli en herlig tid. Vi prøver hardt, kjemper for å komme dit.
Ikke for å ta opp for mye av deres tid, men før jeg drar herfra vil jeg si noen få ord. Jeg tenkte bare for noen øyeblikk siden da jeg kom hit, at noen av mine venner fra Arkansas og andre deler av landet har kommet for å si farvel til oss i morgen. Jeg er virkelig takknemlig for det.
Jeg ser en ung dame ved pianoet. Hun var nettopp på et møte der jeg forkynte, eller prøvde å forkynne. Jeg er ikke en forkynner, men hun mottok Åndens dåp i møtet, og jeg er glad for å se henne.
Bror Moore fortalte meg nettopp at det er flere her fra forskjellige deler av landet, og vi er takknemlige for det. Jeg håper å være tilbake med dere alle en dag.

text:
Now, over in the Book of Saint Luke, the 2nd chapter, I--I wish just to read a few words. I was aiming to come a little early and speak just a little more, but I--I believe that we must read the Word of God each service. See, my words will fail, as all mortals, but God's Word cannot fail. It's forever lasting true.
And just for a short time to speak to you before forming the prayer line, Saint Luke the 2nd chapter, beginning with the 25th verse…
When I look down upon the Bible, I have a small print here, a Scofield Bible. While you all are turning to the chapter; it makes me feel so happy to see the Bible and to be able to read.
One time, I was practically blind. They had to lead me around. My eyesight was gone. I wore great thick glasses. And one moment's time, my eyes changed from blindness to sight, and I can… My eyes test 20/20. And I can read newspaper print five feet from me. That's grace of God that did that.
text_no:
I Lukas' bok, kapittel 2, ønsker jeg å lese noen få ord. Jeg planla å komme litt tidligere for å tale mer utførlig, men jeg tror det er viktig å lese Herrens Ord i hvert møte. Mine ord kan svikte, som alle menneskers, men Guds Ord svikter aldri. Det er evig sant.
Før vi danner bønnekøen, vil jeg tale kort til dere. Vi leser fra Lukas' evangelium, kapittel 2, vers 25.
Når jeg ser ned på Bibelen, har jeg en liten skrift her, en Scofield-Bibel. Mens dere slår opp i kapittelet, gjør det meg så glad å se Bibelen og å kunne lese den.
En gang var jeg nesten blind. De måtte lede meg rundt; synet mitt var borte, og jeg brukte tykke briller. På et øyeblikk gikk jeg fra blindhet til å kunne se klart. Nå tester synet mitt 20/20, og jeg kan lese avistrykk fra fem fot unna. Det var Guds nåde som gjorde det.

text:
If the whole world doubted It, I would still believe It with all my heart. If I would pray for ten thousand people overseas, or here, and all ten thousand would die a moment after I prayed for them, I'd still say Divine healing was right. It's the Word of God. And if I preach to ten thousand, and they all died, and they were dead for twenty years and returned back and said there is no Jesus, there--there is no such a thing as God, there is no eternity, and Jesus wasn't the Son of God, and I was dying, I'd say, "Let me go in Him, I take my bark." No matter what anyone else would say, I believe Him with all my heart. That's right. I believe Him.
In the 25th verse, we read the adoration of prophecy of Simeon.
And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; and the same man was just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parent brought the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
Lord, now lettest thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
For my eyes have seen thy salvation.
text_no:
Selv om hele verden tvilte, ville jeg fortsatt tro med hele mitt hjerte. Om jeg ba for ti tusen mennesker, enten i utlandet eller her, og alle ti tusen døde et øyeblikk etter at jeg ba for dem, ville jeg fortsatt si at guddommelig helbredelse er riktig. Det er Guds Ord. Og om jeg preket for ti tusen, og de alle døde, og hadde vært døde i tjue år og kom tilbake og sa at det ikke finnes noen Jesus, ingen slik ting som Gud, ingen evighet, og at Jesus ikke var Guds Sønn, og jeg holdt på å dø, ville jeg si, "La meg gå i Ham, jeg tar min sjanse." Uansett hva andre ville si, tror jeg på Ham med hele mitt hjerte. Det er riktig. Jeg tror på Ham.
I det 25. verset leser vi om profetien og tilbedelsen til Simeon:
Og se, det var en mann i Jerusalem som het Simeon; og denne mannen var rettferdig og gudfryktig, og ventet på Israels trøst, og Den Hellige Ånd var over ham.
Det var blitt åpenbart for ham ved Den Hellige Ånd at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Herrens Kristus.
Han kom ved Ånden inn i tempelet, og da foreldrene brakte barnet Jesus for å gjøre med ham etter lovens skikk,
da tok han Ham opp i sine armer, priste Gud og sa:
Herre, nå lar Du Din tjener fare i fred, etter Ditt ord,
for mine øyne har sett Din frelse.

text:
Shall we bow our heads just a moment for prayer. Our heavenly Father, as we come into Thy Presence now, dedicating ourselves and this building, and all that's in here for Your service tonight, realizing that this may be the last time that some here will ever hear the Gospel, or be privileged to be in church any more; and this may prove the eternal destination of their being. So help us, Father, tonight, to be very reverent, and to be very loyal to the Gospel, and to the call that Thou has called us into this world. And I thank Thee, Father, tonight for the privilege that I have, to stand before this audience of people.
I've often thought how I would cherish in my hands, a charger that held one drop of the Blood of Thy Son. But I realize tonight I hold greater, in His estimation. Before me is a purchase of His Blood, that He gave His Blood that these might become His.
text_no:
La oss bøye hodene våre et øyeblikk for bønn. Vår himmelske Far, når vi nå trer inn i Din Nærhet, vier vi oss selv og denne bygningen, og alt som er her inne, til Din tjeneste i kveld. Vi er klar over at dette kan være siste gang noen her vil høre Evangeliet, eller få muligheten til å være i menighet noe mer; og dette kan avgjøre deres evige bestemmelse. Så hjelp oss, Far, i kveld, til å være svært ærbødige og lojale overfor Evangeliet og det kall Du har gitt oss i denne verden. Jeg takker Deg, Far, i kveld for privilegiet jeg har, å stå foran dette publikummet.
Jeg har ofte tenkt på hvor høyt jeg ville verdsette i mine hender et beger som holdt en dråpe av Blodet til Din Sønn. Men jeg innser i kveld at jeg holder noe større i Hans øyne. Foran meg er et kjøp av Hans Blod, at Han gav Sitt Blod, slik at disse kunne bli Hans.

text:
And Father, it's my lot tonight to speak to these people. So may Thy Spirit direct every word. And how we thank Thee for the grace of God, to know that one time we were aliens cut off from God without mercy, without hope. Christ died for us in our stead, bearing our reproach and sins in His own body, and knowing someday that He shall come. We do not know now what we shall be like, but we know we'll have a body like His; for we shall see Him as He is. And Father, that'll be a glorified body, free from sickness, and sorrow, and heartaches, and death. Oh, our spirits groan for that deliverance.
And help us tonight, Father, as we're journeying on now, knowing that Thou has provided a way, that the sick could be healed, and the lost could be saved. And as we're gathered here, as eternity bound mortals, help us to set our affections on things above, where Christ sets at the right hand of God. Grant it, Father. And may the Angel of God, Who's guided me through my life, fed me since I was born, be here tonight to heal the sick and afflicted. For we ask it in the Name of Thy beloved Child, Jesus Christ. Amen.
text_no:
Far, i kveld er det min oppgave å tale til disse menneskene. Må Din Ånd lede hvert ord. Vi takker Deg for Guds nåde, for å vite at vi en gang var fremmede, avskåret fra Gud, uten nåde, uten håp. Kristus døde for oss i vårt sted, bar vår skam og våre synder i Sin egen kropp, og vi vet at en dag skal Han komme igjen. Vi vet ikke nå hvordan vi skal være, men vi vet at vi skal ha en kropp som Hans, for vi skal se Ham som Han er. Far, det vil være en herliggjort kropp, fri fra sykdom, sorg, hjertesorg og død. Våre ånder sukker etter den frelsen.
Hjelp oss i kveld, Far, mens vi er på reise, vitende om at Du har gjort en vei, slik at de syke kan bli helbredet, og de fortapte kan bli frelst. Og mens vi er samlet her, som evighetsbundne dødelige, hjelp oss å sette våre hjerter på ting ovenfor, der Kristus sitter ved Guds høyre hånd. Innvilg det, Far. Og må Guds Engel, som har ledet meg gjennom livet og mettet meg siden jeg ble født, være her i kveld for å helbrede de syke og lidende. For vi ber om det i Din elskede Sønns, Jesu Kristi, Navn. Amen.

text:
Just for a little text, if I should call it that, I want to speak for a moment or two on the word of "Expectation."
We always get what we expect. When people expect anything, well then they usually get what you look for. It's the mental attitude that you have.
Remember this, and never forget it. The right mental attitude towards any promise of God will bring it to pass, the right mental attitude towards any promise of God.
It doesn't take gifts of healing to heal sick people, any person in here has a right to meet Satan anywhere, if you're a Christian, and defeat him on any grounds that he could stand on.
When Jesus Christ was here on earth, and in Him dwelled the fullness of the Godhead bodily, all the great powers of God was in His Son, Christ Jesus. For the Scripture tells us that God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself. All things was delivered unto Him, though He spoke nothing but what the Father told Him. He healed no one but what He first saw being healed. For He said, "Verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing in Himself, but what He sees the Father doing." That was Saint John 5:19.
text_no:
For en kort tekst, om jeg kan kalle det det, vil jeg gjerne snakke litt om Ordet "Forventning."
Vi får alltid det vi forventer. Når folk forventer noe, får de som regel det de ser etter. Det er den mentale holdningen du har.
Husk dette, og glem det aldri: Den rette mentale holdningen mot enhver av Guds løfter vil oppfylle det, den rette mentale holdningen mot ethvert av Guds løfter.
Det krever ikke helbredelsesgaver for å helbrede syke mennesker. Enhver person her inne har rett til å møte Satan hvor som helst, hvis du er en kristen, og beseire ham på hvilket som helst grunnlag han måtte stå på.
Da Jesus Kristus var her på jorden, og i Ham bodde hele Guds fylde legemlig, var alle Guds store krefter i Hans Sønn, Kristus Jesus. For Skriften forteller oss at Gud var i Kristus og forsonte verden med Seg Selv. Alt ble overgitt til Ham, selv om Han ikke talte noe annet enn det Faderen fortalte Ham. Han helbredet ingen uten at Han først så det bli helbredet. For Han sa: "Sannelig, jeg sier dere: Sønnen kan ikke gjøre noe av Seg Selv, men bare det Han ser Faderen gjøre." Dette står i Johannes 5:19.

text:
Saint John 5:1, He passed by the pool of Bethesda, and there was great multitudes of lame, halt, blind, withered, waiting for the moving of the water. And when the waters would be troubled, then the first person stepping into the water, by faith, received healing. They had to believe it. And then, the virtue of the Angel went into the person, they were healed. And then the people waited for another season, maybe a week or two, month or two. And they laid there, great multitudes of people.
Jesus passing by here seen a man, not crippled, but with an infirmity for thirty and eight years. And He said, "Would thou be made whole?"
And he said, "I have no one to put me in the water."
He said, "Take up thy bed and go into the house."
And the same was a Sabbath day, but the man picked up his bed and obeyed. And when he was questioned, in the 19th verse of the same chapter, by the Jewish people, the priests of that day, He said, "The Son can do nothing in Himself, but what He sees the Father doing."
text_no:
I Johannes 5:1 passerer Jesus ved Betesdas dam, hvor det var store folkemengder av lamme, halte, blinde og visne som ventet på at vannet skulle bevege seg. Når vannet ble opprørt, mottok den første personen som trådte ut i det, i tro, helbredelse. De måtte tro det. Deretter gikk dyden fra Engelen inn i personen, og de ble helbredet. Folket ventet så på en ny anledning, kanskje en uke eller to, en måned eller to. Det var store folkemengder som lå der.
Jesus, som passerer her, så en mann som ikke var lam, men som hadde en sykdom i trettifire år. Han sa: "Vil du bli helbredet?"
Mannen svarte: "Jeg har ingen til å hjelpe meg ned i vannet."
Jesus sa da: "Ta opp din seng og gå hjem."
Dette skjedde på en sabbat, men mannen tok opp sengen og adlød. Da han ble utspurt i det 19. verset av samme kapittel av de jødiske lederne, presten i den tid, svarte Han: "Sønnen kan ikke gjøre noe av Seg selv, men hva Han ser Faderen gjøre."

text:
It's always foreseen, what is going to take place by gifts in Spirit. It's the revelation of God that's revealed. And therefore, you only act and dramatize what you've already seen happen.
Many people gets it mixed up sometimes about gifts and callings. Gifts and callings are without repentance. God foreordains those things and sends them on the earth. And then a vindicates them that they are.
Here not long ago, a person walked to me. Now… And many times they do, and said, "THUS SAITH THE LORD, The Lord said, 'Do this.' If you didn't go to a certain place, or a certain thing, and do a certain thing, the Lord's going to strike you and take this gift away from you."
text_no:
Alt som skal skje, er forutsett gjennom Guds gaver i Ånden. Det er Guds åpenbaring som blir avslørt. Derfor handler og dramatiserer du bare det du allerede har sett skje. 
Mange mennesker blander iblant sammen gaver og kall. Gaver og kall er uten anger. Gud forutbestemmer disse tingene og sender dem til jorden. Han stadfester dem slik at de blir bevist som gyldige.
For ikke lenge siden kom en person bort til meg. Dette skjer mange ganger. Vedkommende sa, "SÅ SIER HERREN, Herren sa, 'Gjør dette.' Hvis du ikke drar til et bestemt sted, eller utfører en bestemt handling, vil Herren slå deg og ta bort denne gaven fra deg."

text:
Now, that isn't true. That isn't true. Gifts and callings are without repentance. There is spirits that's in the Church. That's true, such as gifts of prophecy, healing, and so forth. But they're in the entire body. They may fall upon one person tonight, a prophecy, and It might not never fall on that person again; It might be somewhere else in the body. And those spirits are supposed to be judged. "Let one speak and two or three judge," said Paul.
Now, those are the spirits of the gifts that's in the Church. But the only person that has a right to say, THUS SAITH THE LORD, is an a vindicated prophet. You never seen anybody judging Isaiah, or Jeremiah, or those people. They were prophets, foreordained and borned in the world to be prophets. And they foresaw the thing by a vision, and then said, "THUS SAITH THE LORD," for the Lord had already said it.
So what the Church needs today, is a good old fashion teaching of the Gospel. That's right. To know where they… I don't mean just educated teaching; I mean spiritual teaching. Some people speaks about God, and--and knows nothing of Him. You don't… See, you must get down and deal with the actual spirit itself, you see, to know what you're speaking of.
text_no:
Det er ikke sant. Gaver og kall er uten anger. Det finnes ånder i menigheten, slik som gaver til profeti, helbredelse og lignende. Men de er i hele kroppen. De kan falle på én person i kveld med en profeti, og den personen kan aldri oppleve det igjen; det kan opptre et annet sted i kroppen. Disse åndene skal prøves. «La én tale og to eller tre dømme,» sa Paulus.
Dette er gavens ånder som er i menigheten. Men den eneste som har rett til å si, SLIK SIER HERREN, er en stadfestet profet. Ingen dømte Jesaja, Jeremias eller andre som var profeter. De var profeter, forutbestemt og født til å være profeter. De forutså ting gjennom en visjon og sa deretter, "SLIK SIER HERREN," fordi Herren allerede hadde sagt det.
Det menigheten trenger i dag, er en god, gammeldags undervisning av evangeliet. Det er riktig. Ikke bare utdannet undervisning, men åndelig undervisning. Noen taler om Gud uten å kjenne Ham. Man må komme ned og forholde seg til selve ånden for å vite hva man taler om.

text:
And now, Simeon was a man, a great man, led by the Holy Spirit. Do you believe in being led by the Holy Spirit? Certainly. We all do, that's Christians, believe that the Holy Spirit leads us.
And Simeon was a great man. He was an honorable man, a priest; and late years of eighty, as we're told. And he stayed in the temple.
And remember, he had a lot of prestige on his name, of being a priest, or teacher there in--in Israel. But it was revealed to him by the Holy Spirit, that he was not going to see death until first he seen Christ. And he was not afraid to make that statement to all the people. No matter what his name was, or his prestige was, or his title, the Holy Spirit had revealed to him that he was not going to see death until first he seen Christ.
text_no:
Simeon var en betydningsfull mann, ledet av Den Hellige Ånd. Tror du på å bli ledet av Den Hellige Ånd? Det gjør vi alle som er kristne, vi tror at Den Hellige Ånd leder oss.
Simeon var en stor mann, en ærefull mann, en prest; i sine sene år, rundt åtti, som vi er blitt fortalt. Han oppholdt seg i tempelet.
Husk at han hadde stor prestisje som prest eller lærer i Israel. Men det var åpenbart for ham av Den Hellige Ånd at han ikke ville se døden før han hadde sett Kristus. Han var ikke redd for å uttale dette til folket. Uansett navn, prestisje eller tittel hadde Den Hellige Ånd åpenbart for ham at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Kristus.

text:
Now, look. No one had ever seen Christ. He had been spoke of it from the very dawn of time, from the beginning in the garden of Eden. And four thousand years had passed, and every great man had looked for Him to come on the earth. The Jewish stream of blood, I guess, is the most purest there is on earth tonight, looking for the Messiah to come. And they--they watched for that Child to be born. They were looking for It.
And so therefore, all of them had looked. But Simeon had a revelation of the Holy Spirit, that he was not going to see death until first he saw Christ. He was not going to die until he saw Christ. And he told everyone that that's what was going to happen. Not afraid that it wouldn't happen, he knowed if the Holy Spirit said so, it had to happen.
When God tells you anything, it's positive the truth. You cannot get away from it, and it's got to happen.
text_no:
Se nå. Ingen hadde noen gang sett Kristus. Han hadde blitt omtalt helt fra tidens morgen, fra begynnelsen i Edens hage. Fire tusen år hadde gått, og alle store menn hadde ventet på Hans komme til jorden. Den jødiske blodlinjen, antar jeg, er den reneste som finnes på jorden i dag, i påvente av Messias. De ventet på at Barnet skulle fødes. De lette etter Ham.
Alle hadde ventet, men Simeon hadde fått en åpenbaring fra Den Hellige Ånd. Han skulle ikke se døden før han hadde sett Kristus. Han skulle ikke dø før han hadde sett Kristus, og han fortalte alle at det var slik det skulle skje. Han fryktet ikke at det ikke skulle skje, fordi han visste at hvis Den Hellige Ånd hadde sagt det, måtte det skje.
Når Gud forteller deg noe, er det positivt sant. Du kan ikke unnslippe det, og det må skje.

text:
Someone said, "Brother Branham, aren't you afraid you'll make a mistake when you're… someone comes to the platform, that you'll tell them the wrong disease?" No sir.
"Aren't you afraid…"
Here the other day, there's a man arrested down in the south for telling a man he lived untrue to his wife, and so forth, and was arrested, 'cause it was a story. Why sure, if it's a story, anything can happen. You're working in yourself. But when you stand under the Divine promise of God, there's no fear in God. That's right. It's bound to be. It's perfect. It's never failed, and it will never fail, because God cannot lie.
You can only believe God when He speaks and tells you, then you act upon what He says do.
text_no:
Noen spurte, "Bror Branham, er du ikke redd for å gjøre en feil når du… når noen kommer til plattformen, at du vil fortelle dem feil sykdom?" Nei, sir.
"Er du ikke redd…"
Her om dagen ble en mann arrestert i sør for å ha fortalt en annen mann at han var utro mot sin kone, og ble arrestert fordi det var en usann historie. Naturligvis, hvis det er en løgn, kan hva som helst skje. Da arbeider du på egen hånd. Men når du står under Guds guddommelige løfte, er det ingen frykt i Gud. Det er riktig. Det må være slik. Det er perfekt. Det har aldri sviktet, og det vil aldri svikte, fordi Gud ikke kan lyve.
Du kan bare tro på Gud når Han taler og forteller deg noe, og deretter handler du i henhold til det Han sier du skal gjøre.

text:
Now, this priest wasn't ashamed to tell the people that he believed that Jesus was, or Christ was going to be born, and he would see It before he died.
Now, notice. When God makes a promise to you, He will fulfill it. You don't have to be afraid that He will not keep His Word, for God will keep His Word. I… That's true. You just must trust Him.
text_no:
Denne presten skammet seg ikke over å fortelle folket at han trodde at Jesus, eller Kristus, skulle bli født, og at han ville se Ham før han døde.
Legg merke til dette: Når Gud gir deg et løfte, vil Han oppfylle det. Du trenger ikke være redd for at Han ikke skal holde sitt Ord, for Gud vil alltid holde Sitt Ord. Det er sant. Du må bare stole på Ham.

text:
And then I notice this: My, when Jesus was born then, why there… They didn't have the radio and press, as we have today. They just mouth to ear, the way they had to bring message.
And eight days later, Mary brought the little child Jesus to the temple to do as the custom of the law, to offer turtledoves, or--or pigeons if they were poor, and so forth that they was to make the sacrifice, the purification of her.
And notice, all this just gets right next to me when I think of it. Now, Simeon, probably out somewhere in a prayer room, or outside the temple. And maybe that morning, there would probably be fifty children, we'd say, standing in the line for circumcision.
But notice, just when Mary brought Jesus in, the Holy Spirit spoke to Simeon somewhere else outside. And he was led by the Holy Spirit right straight to the Christ Child. Come in at that very instant, picked Him up in his arms, and said, "Lord, let Your servant depart in peace, for I've seen Your salvation." Think of it.
text_no:
Og så legger jeg merke til dette: Når Jesus ble født, hvorfor... De hadde ikke radio og presse som vi har i dag. De måtte bringe budskapet fra munn til øre.
Åtte dager senere brakte Maria den lille Jesus til tempelet for å oppfylle lovens skikk, for å ofre turtelduer eller--eller duer hvis de var fattige, og så videre, for å gjøre ofringen og renselsen for henne.
Og legg merke til, alt dette går rett inn på meg når jeg tenker på det. Simeon var sannsynligvis et sted i et bønnerom, eller utenfor tempelet. Kanskje den morgenen var det femti barn i kø for omskjæring.
Men legg merke til, akkurat når Maria kom med Jesus, talte Den Hellige Ånd til Simeon et annet sted. Han ble ledet av Den Hellige Ånd rett til Kristusbarnet. Kom inn akkurat i det øyeblikket, tok Ham opp i armene sine og sa: "Herre, la Din tjener fare herfra i fred, for jeg har sett Din frelse." Tenk på det.

text:
Now, he had the promise. And isn't it strange, if the Holy Spirit give him that promise, and when the promise came, the Holy Spirit led Simeon, the one that had the promise, right straight to It. See how He does it?
Now, I do not believe there's anyone here… How many people in here is sick tonight, wants to be prayed for, let's see your hands, over the building? I don't believe you come to be seen. I don't believe you come to criticize. I believe you come, because you're expecting to be healed. And you believe in healing, don't you? You believe in Divine healing. And the very reason that you believe in Divine healing, there's something in you that makes you believe in Divine healing.
And isn't it just the same Holy Spirit tonight, if He promised He would send in the last days these things? Did He promise He would send this in the last days, "and it shall come to pass in the last days," saith God? How He promised in Mark 16, "Go ye into all the world…" That's New York. See? "Preach the Gospel to every creature. These signs shall follow them that believe." He promised it. And then He promised the former rain and the latter rain. And we're living now, have been for years, under the latter rain.
text_no:
Nå hadde han løftet. Er det ikke merkelig, hvis Den Hellige Ånd ga ham løftet, og når løftet kom, ledet Den Hellige Ånd Simeon, den som hadde løftet, rett til Det. Ser du hvordan Han gjør det?
Nå, jeg tror ikke det er noen her... Hvor mange her er syke i kveld og ønsker forbønn? La meg se hendene deres rundt i bygget. Jeg tror ikke dere kom for å bli sett. Jeg tror ikke dere kom for å kritisere. Jeg tror dere kom fordi dere forventer å bli helbredet. Og dere tror på helbredelse, gjør dere ikke? Dere tror på guddommelig helbredelse. Og grunnen til at dere tror på guddommelig helbredelse, er at det er noe i dere som får dere til å tro på guddommelig helbredelse.
Og er det ikke den samme Hellige Ånd i kveld, hvis Han lovte at Han ville sende disse tingene i de siste dager? Sa Han ikke at Han ville sende dette i de siste dager, "og det skal skje i de siste dager," sier Gud? Hvordan Han lovte i Markus 16: "Gå ut i all verden..." Det er New York. Ser du? "Forkynn evangeliet for alle skapninger. Disse tegn skal følge dem som tror." Han lovte det. Og så lovte Han tidligere regn og den senere regn. Og vi lever nå, og har gjort det i år, under den senere regn.

text:
And just before God cut off relationship with the Jews, He put nine spiritual gifts into the Church. And just before the Gentile's age is over, He's restoring back in the Church those nine spiritual gifts, giving the Gentile church its last call before turning again to Israel.
And now tonight, while these gifts are promised, and you're sick, don't you think that the Holy Spirit led you here tonight just the same as It did Simeon to Jesus when He came? The same Holy Spirit leading… The very means of you believing in healing, it shows that there's something back there making you believe in healing.
Here, before there is a desire created in your heart, there has to be a Creator to create that creation. Isn't that right?
In other words like this. Before there was a fin on a fish's back, there had to be a water first for him to swim in, or he would not have had any fin. See what I mean?
Before there was a tree to grow in the earth, there had to be an earth first for it to grow in, or there would not been no tree.
And if you have a desire in your heart to be healed by Divine healing, there's bound to be a Fountain of healing somewhere, or that desire wouldn't be in your heart.
text_no:
Rett før Gud brøt forholdet til jødene, plasserte Han ni åndelige gaver i Menigheten. Og rett før hedningenes tidsalder er over, gjenoppretter Han disse ni åndelige gavene i Menigheten, og gir hedningemenigheten dens siste kall før Han igjen vender seg til Israel.
Og nå i kveld, mens disse gavene er lovet og du er syk, tror du ikke at Den Hellige Ånd ledet deg hit i kveld, akkurat som Den ledet Simeon til Jesus da Han kom? Den samme Hellige Ånd leder... Selve faktum at du tror på helbredelse viser at det er noe som får deg til å ha tro på helbredelse.
Før et ønske skapes i ditt hjerte, må det være en Skaper som skaper det ønsket. Er ikke det riktig?
Med andre ord, som dette: Før det fantes en finne på en fiskerygg, måtte det først være vann for den å svømme i, ellers ville den ikke hatt noen finne. Forstår du hva jeg mener?
Før det var et tre som kunne vokse i jorden, måtte det først være jord for det å vokse i, ellers ville det ikke vært noe tre.
Og hvis du har et ønske i ditt hjerte om å bli helbredet ved guddommelig helbredelse, må det være en Høgkilde av helbredelse et sted, ellers ville ikke det ønsket vært i ditt hjerte.

text:
If there's a desire to worship God… Even the Hottentots and Indians in years gone by, they worshipped objects, because the very creation of worship was in their heart, and they worship something. They worshipped something as their Creator. And that showed that there was a Creator somewhere to worship, a God.
If there's a hunger in your heart for more of God, there's bound to be more of God somewhere for you to find.
text_no:
Hvis det er et ønske om å tilbe Gud... Selv hottentotter og indianere i tidligere tider tilba objekter, fordi behovet for tilbedelse var i deres hjerte, og de tilba noe. De tilba noe som sin Skaper. Dette viste at det fantes en Skaper et sted å tilbe, en Gud.
Hvis det er en hunger i ditt hjerte etter mer av Gud, er det sikkert mer av Gud et sted for deg å finne.

text:
My mother used to have a superstition. The little children that I… had ten of us. And when the little fellows would be… They'd get a habit of licking their lips. And she's say they were tasting for something. And she'd put, maybe preserves on. That didn't work. Maybe put a little honey, that didn't work. Maybe some watermelon, that didn't work. But after a while, when she found something that they were tasting for, why, they were… It would stop licking its lips.
Well, that might have been a superstition. But this, when there's a hunger in the heart of the human being for something, there's bound to be Something creating that hunger in there.
In other words, David said, "When the deep calls to the deep…" And when there's a deep calling, there's bound to be a deep to respond somewhere. And if there's a deep calling for more of God for healing, there's bound to be a fountain of healing and the power of God somewhere to respond. Do you believe that? With all my heart, I know it's the truth. And as we call, and there's something in us tells us there's a land beyond the river.
text_no:
Min mor hadde en overtro. Vi var ti barn, og når de små begynte å slikke leppene, sa hun at de smakte etter noe. Hun prøvde å sette på syltetøy, honning, vannmelon, men ingenting fungerte. Etter en stund, når hun fant det de egentlig smakte etter, sluttet de å slikke leppene.
Det var kanskje overtro, men når det er en sult i hjertet til et menneske etter noe, må det være Noe som skaper den sulten.
David sa, "Når dypet kaller på dypet…" Når det er et dypt rop etter mer av Gud, må det finnes en kilde til helbredelse og Guds kraft et sted som svarer. Tror du ikke det? Av hele mitt hjerte vet jeg at det er sant. Når vi roper, og noe inni oss forteller oss at det er et land bortenfor elven, da er det slik.

text:
I buried a wife and baby some fifteen years ago, and standing by the grave when looked like everything was around me, my whole family gone. And they was sprinkling flowers upon the little baby's casket, died a few hours after its mother. And my heart was broken; I could not stand seemed like, any longer. I heard the minister say, "Ashes to ashes, dust to dust, and earth to earth."
And it seemed like coming down through the trees, there was a breeze come saying,
There's a land beyond the river.
That we call the sweet forever,
We only reach that shore by faith's decree;
One by one we gain the portal,
There to dwell with the immortal,
Someday they'll ring the golden bells for you and me.
text_no:
Jeg begravde en kone og et barn for rundt femten år siden, og der jeg sto ved graven, føltes det som om hele verden raste sammen rundt meg. Hele familien min var borte. De strødde blomster på den lille babyens kiste, som døde noen timer etter moren. Hjertet mitt var knust; det føltes som om jeg ikke kunne holde ut lenger. Jeg hørte presten si: "Aske til aske, støv til støv, og jord til jord."
Og det virket som om det kom en bris gjennom trærne, som sa:
Det finnes en land på andre siden av elven,
Som vi kaller det søte for evig,
Vi når kun den kysten ved troens dekret;
En etter en når vi portalen,
For å bo med de udødelige,
En dag vil de ringe de gyldne klokker for deg og meg.

text:
[Blank.spot.on.tape.]… something calling out, a deep. There's a greater place, a better land. See what I mean? I'm expecting that to be there. I'm expecting God when I was praying in the room a--a few moments ago before entering the building, seemed like Something come over me that said something was going to happen here tonight, a great spontaneous healing. I'm expecting that. God has…
I will venturely say this to you pastors. You'll find out that at least sixty to eighty percent of every sick person in this building will be healed in the next hour. I… See if that isn't right, you can brand me a false prophet. I know what I am speaking of. That's right.
text_no:
[Tomt.område.på.lydbånd]… noe kaller ut, en dyp følelse. Det finnes et større sted, et bedre land. Forstår dere hva jeg mener? Jeg forventer at det er der. Jeg forventer at Gud, da jeg ba på rommet for noen øyeblikk siden før jeg kom inn i bygningen, det virket som om Noe kom over meg som sa at noe stort skulle skje her i kveld, en stor spontan helbredelse. Jeg forventer det. Gud har...
Jeg vil våge å si dette til dere pastorer. Dere vil se at minst seksti til åtti prosent av alle syke i denne bygningen vil bli helbredet i løpet av den neste timen. Om det ikke stemmer, kan dere merke meg som en falsk profet. Jeg vet hva jeg snakker om. Det er riktig.

text:
Now, I--I believe it. I prayed, and I asked God if He would just move over the people in this service, and somehow… 'cause I only get to so many. But I asked the Father if He would just… And when I was praying, Something soothed down over me. I knew then that God was going to answer my prayer. I do not know what will happen, but there'll be something that'll happen that will--will do it. I want you to constantly be in prayer.
Now, notice. When you're expecting something, Simeon was expecting Jesus to come while he was alive. He was expecting it, and God rewarded him for his faith. I'm expecting God to heal at least sixty or eighty percent of the people in here tonight, perfectly well before this service is over. I believe it with all my soul. See? I'm expecting it.
text_no:
Jeg tror på dette. Jeg ba og spurte Gud om Han ville bevege seg over folket i dette møtet, på en måte... for jeg rekker ikke å nå fram til alle. Jeg ba Faderen om Han kunne... Og da jeg ba, kjente jeg en beroligende følelse komme over meg. Da visste jeg at Gud ville svare på bønnen min. Jeg vet ikke hva som vil skje, men noe vil skje som vil ordne det. Jeg ønsker at dere skal være i konstant bønn.
Legg merke til dette. Når du forventer noe, var Simeon sikker på at Jesus ville komme mens han levde. Han forventet det, og Gud belønnet ham for sin tro. Jeg forventer at Gud skal helbrede minst seksti eller åtti prosent av menneskene her i kveld, fullstendig, før dette møtet er over. Jeg tror det med hele mitt hjerte. Se? Jeg forventer det.

text:
Daniel was expecting God to deliver him from the lion's den.
The Hebrew children, when they went in the fiery furnace, they said, "Our God is able…" They was expecting God to deliver them. And they made the last step of the way before He came, but He's always there at the last end. Jesus comes in the darkest of hours, then Jesus comes along. They was expecting it.
The woman with the blood issue was expecting. If she could touch the hem of His garment, it was over. When she touched the hem of His garment, her expectations were fulfilled.
If you come tonight expecting to be healed, you're going to be healed. If you come tonight expecting to find something to criticize the meeting, you'll certainly find it. You get what you come for. That's right.
text_no:
Daniel forventet at Gud ville fri ham fra løvehulen. De hebraiske barna, da de gikk inn i ildovnen, sa: "Vår Gud er i stand..." De forventet at Gud skulle fri dem. De tok det siste skrittet før Han kom, men Han er alltid der til slutt. Jesus kommer i de mørkeste timer. De forventet det.
Kvinnen med blodsykdommen forventet også. Hun tenkte at hvis hun kunne berøre kanten av Hans kappe, ville hun bli helbredet. Da hun rørte ved kanten av Hans kappe, ble hennes forventninger innfridd.
Hvis du kommer i kveld og forventer helbredelse, vil du bli helbredet. Hvis du kommer i kveld og forventer å finne noe å kritisere ved møtene, vil du sikkert finne det. Du får det du kommer for. Det er riktig.

text:
Notice. Blind Bartimaeus, he was expecting. If he could get to Jesus, he, or attract His attention, that he would be healed. So they tried to get him to set down, but he cried out the more, "Thou Son of David, have mercy on me," for he knew if he could ever bring the attention of Jesus to him, that he expected Jesus would heal him.
If you're expecting to be healed tonight, you will be healed. Whatever your expectations are…
Just the thought of my inside life… I'm leaving now after a bit to cross over to the other country. And I'm going to tell you just a little thing. There's things that goes along in this, friends, that no one knows about. That's right. I do not tell the--the people all the things that God would say. Everyone has that in their life. That's your private affair between you and God.
text_no:
Legg merke til blinde Bartimeus; han hadde forventninger. Hvis han kunne komme til Jesus eller fange Hans oppmerksomhet, trodde han at han ville bli helbredet. De prøvde å få ham til å sette seg ned, men han ropte enda høyere: "Du Davids Sønn, ha barmhjertighet med meg," for han visste at hvis han kunne tiltrekke seg Jesu oppmerksomhet, forventet han at Jesus ville helbrede ham.
Hvis du forventer å bli helbredet i kveld, vil du bli helbredet. Uansett hva dine forventninger er...
Bare tanken på mitt indre liv... Jeg skal snart reise til et annet land. Og jeg vil fortelle dere en liten ting. Det er ting som skjer i dette, venner, som ingen vet om. Det stemmer. Jeg forteller ikke folk alt det Gud vil si. Alle har det slik i sitt liv. Det er en privat sak mellom deg og Gud.

text: But this has been a glorious thing if I never come back. I feel that God has vindicated and proven that I have told the truth to the nation if I never return again. That's right. He's scientifically proven it. And He's proved it over and over that I have told the truth. That Spirit leads. He does things that… Many times in the room, I--I'll be in there praying and see things in the meeting that's just going to take place, and call my managers and things together, and tell them of things that's going to happen right in the meeting hours before it ever happens, sometimes weeks before it happens. And certain things… Not as I know it, but God shows it to me, and I know it's got to be so. I believe it.
text_no: Dette har vært en herlig opplevelse, selv om jeg aldri skulle komme tilbake. Jeg føler at Gud har bekreftet og bevist at jeg har fortalt sannheten til nasjonen, selv om jeg aldri skulle vende tilbake. Det stemmer. Han har vitenskapelig bevist det. Og Han har bevist det gang på gang at jeg har fortalt sannheten. Den Ånden leder. Han gjør ting som… Mange ganger, mens jeg er i rommet og ber, ser jeg hendelser som vil finne sted på møtet. Jeg kaller sammen lederne mine og forteller dem hva som vil skje, ofte flere timer før det skjer, noen ganger uker i forveien. Det er ikke fordi jeg vet det, men fordi Gud viser det til meg, og jeg vet at det må være slik. Jeg tror på det.

text:
Here not long ago in Fort Wayne, we were having a meeting, a glorious meeting there where Paul Rader used to preach, B. E. Rediger, many of you knows those national, international known ministers. And I was setting in the room when they was singing, "Only Believe" in the very room that Paul Rader wrote the song, a few months ago. And we were staying at the Indiana Hotel. Many times, they don't let the people know where I am.
Now, that's what's hard to me, to have to pass through people that I know that love me. And I--I just… My heart cries for them. But you--you… There's so many. It just takes the very life. It's…
When them vibrations hit the hand here sometime, you watch my brother and them. They watch me. They know when I have enough; they'll grab me and run from the platform. Sometimes, friends, I pass completely out, and they ride me around over the city maybe for two or three hours trying to get me to myself. It just depletes my strength so bad.
text_no:
Nylig i Fort Wayne hadde vi et møte, et herlig møte på stedet hvor Paul Rader pleide å forkynne. Mange av dere kjenner til B. E. Rediger og andre nasjonalt og internasjonalt kjente forkynnere. Jeg satt i rommet da de sang "Only Believe" i det samme rommet hvor Paul Rader skrev sangen for noen måneder siden. Vi bodde på Indiana Hotel. Ofte informerer de ikke folk om hvor jeg befinner meg.
Dette er vanskelig for meg, å måtte gå forbi mennesker som jeg vet elsker meg. Mitt hjerte blør for dem. Men det er så mange. Det tar virkelig kraften fra meg.
Når vibrasjonene treffer hånden min, så ser min bror og de andre etter meg. De vet når jeg har fått nok; da griper de tak i meg og tar meg bort fra plattformen. Noen ganger mister jeg helt bevisstheten, og de kjører meg rundt i byen i to til tre timer for at jeg skal komme til meg selv igjen. Det tapper kreftene mine fullstendig.

text:
You might think that that's strange. You might think that it's--that it's odd for something like that, a gift, but it pulls from you. It takes away from you. It's not like preaching the Gospel, or just laying hands on the sick, it's a Divine Being working through a mortal body. I've had my hand to swell till I'd hold it under a hot water for practically a hour to bring feelings back in my hand from the vibration of those hideous cancers and things, how they work. And hardly no one knows those things. My managers and so forth do.
But I remember in this meeting, they had found out where I was staying. And the next day, why, it was pathetic to see the people.
text_no:
Du tenker kanskje at det er rart eller merkelig med en slik gave, men det tapper deg for krefter. Det tar noe fra deg. Det er ikke som å forkynne Evangeliet eller legge hendene på de syke. Det er et Guddommelig Vesen som arbeider gjennom en dødelig kropp. Jeg har opplevd at hånden min har hovnet opp så mye at jeg må holde den under varmt vann i nesten en time for å få tilbake følelsen etter vibrasjonene fra de avskyelige kreftsvulstene og andre sykdommer. Nesten ingen kjenner til disse tingene, unntatt lederne mine og noen få andre.
Jeg husker et møte hvor de hadde funnet ut hvor jeg bodde. Dagen etter var det hjerteskjærende å se menneskene som samlet seg der.

text:
I remember in one meeting in--over in another meeting I was at, I had one night's service, over in Missouri at a--quite a large city. And while I left the auditorium, it was small, just about--audience just about like this tonight. And I went up into a--the third floor. Walked from the--from the tabernacle, or auditorium, up into the third floor of the hotel, and just was lying down asleep just a little bit with my clothes on across the bed. And I was to meet my wife the next day at Poplar Bluff, and was have a radio broadcast, and going on down into Arkansas.
And I remember while lying there about ten minutes, I heard someone knock on the door. And when I went to the door, it was the manager of the hotel. He said, "Are you Reverend Branham?"
I said, "Yes sir."
He said, "Well, Mister Branham, you'll have to leave the hotel."
Well, I said, "What have I done, sir?"
He said, "Well, we can't have this around the hotel." And he opened up the door, and friends, there was about a four lane prayer line come from the door down through, out into the lobby, and down into the street of people followed over there. And I looked out there. He said…
I said, "Well, I don't know what to do, sir."
He said, "You go down the fire escape, and I'll go down and call you a cab. He will come down through the alley and pick you up."
And I went down there, and it was snowing. I looked outside, I seen those little old mothers out there holding things over their babies to keep that snow out of their face, and people on canes, and them with palsy shaking. I just couldn't pass by there.
text_no:
Jeg husker et møte jeg holdt i Missouri, i en relativt stor by, hvor jeg hadde en natt med samling. Publikum var omtrent på samme størrelse som i kveld. Etter møtet dro jeg opp til tredje etasje av hotellet. Jeg gikk fra auditoriet til tredje etasje og lå rett ut med klærne på tvers over sengen, mens jeg småsov. Jeg skulle møte min kone dagen etter i Poplar Bluff, ha en radiosending og reise videre til Arkansas.
Mens jeg lå der i omtrent ti minutter, hørte jeg en banke på døren. Da jeg åpnet, var det hotellets bestyrer. Han spurte: "Er du Pastor Branham?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja, det er jeg."
Han sa: "Vel, Mister Branham, du må forlate hotellet."
Forvirret spurte jeg: "Hva har jeg gjort galt, sir?"
Han svarte: "Vi kan ikke ha dette rundt hotellet." Da han åpnet døren, så jeg en firefelts bønnekø som strakte seg fra døren og ned gjennom lobbyen, videre ut på gaten. Jeg kikket ut og så mange mennesker som hadde fulgt meg hit.
Jeg spurte: "Hva skal jeg gjøre, sir?"
Han sa: "Du kan gå ned nødutgangen. Jeg skal ringe etter en drosje som vil hente deg i bakgata."
Jeg gikk ned og så ut. Det snødde. Utenfor sto mødre som holdt tepper over barna sine for å beskytte dem mot snøen, folk på krykker, og andre med lammelser som ristet. Jeg kunne ikke bare gå forbi dem.

text: I went on out there on the street and started a prayer line. Turned around with street begin to be crowded. And the Lord healing, throwing down their crutches and things, running away. And the cops was all out there directing the traffic. We had a glorious time out there, because that the people believed that they would be healed.
text_no: Jeg gikk ut på gaten og startet en bønnekø. Gaten begynte å bli fylt med folk. Herren helbredet dem, de kastet fra seg krykkene og løp av gårde. Politiet var der og dirigerte trafikken. Vi hadde en herlig tid der ute, fordi folk trodde at de ville bli helbredet.

text:
Now, at Fort Wayne, they found out where the hotel we were in, the Indiana Hotel. And then in that one night, we started down. We just couldn't get out. You just--just people just so congested. It was terrible. And then we had about three more days to stay.
And so the bellhop… My brother hired the bellhop. And he told him which way we go down, and go over the… through the furnace room and come out up in the alley. And so it was hard to do. But they taken me down, go on to the meetings, and go up through the alley; wife, and one of Mayo's nurses that was healed with cancer. And she was with us.
And so they--they was taking me over for breakfast, and I'd been eating at the little place called Toddle House, I believe it was. And… No, Hobb's House. And one morning, we were going down, the second morning, I had my overcoat pulled up, and--and was walking down the street packing my little girl. And all of a sudden, the Holy Spirit came down. Do you believe in being led of the Spirit? And I hand the baby over to the wife, and she said, "What's the matter, honey?"
I said, "The Holy Spirit's leading me."
text_no:
I Fort Wayne fant de ut hvor vi bodde, på Indiana Hotel. En kveld prøvde vi å komme oss ut, men det var så folksomt at vi ikke klarte det. Vi hadde tre dager igjen å være der.
Min bror hyret en pikkolo som hjalp oss med å finne en vei gjennom fyrrommet og opp gjennom bakgården. Selv om det var vanskelig, klarte de å få meg til møtene gjennom denne ruten. Min kone og en av de sykepleierne fra Mayo Clinic, som var blitt helbredet for kreft, var med oss.
En morgen skulle vi over til frokost. Jeg hadde spist på et sted kalt Hobb's House. Den andre morgenen hadde jeg frakken trukket opp rundt meg og bar min lille datter. Plutselig kom Den Hellige Ånd over meg. Tror du på å bli ledet av Ånden? Jeg overleverte barnet til min kone, som spurte: "Hva er det, kjære?"
Jeg svarte: "Den Hellige Ånd leder meg."

text:
And I felt the Angel of the Lord… You've seen the picture. That just came down. And you just do what It says do. And I thought we was going right straight to the Hobb's House, we was about a city block from it. And the Spirit of the Lord said, "Turn to your left."
And I went down, and just kept on walking. They were following me. And I stopped to a place said Miller's Cafeteria. And I went down in the basement, like, where the cafeteria was. And just as… I got some prunes, I believe, and some toast, and was setting down to eat. And I heard somebody say, "Praise the Lord."
I looked over, and a lady just raised up, and the tears running down her cheeks. And Mrs. Morgan, the nurse, said, "See? You're caught now." See?
And I said, "Just a moment."
The lady walked over, she said, "Brother Branham." She said, "I've followed you meeting after meeting." She said, "I have a brother here. His heart's broke through the diaphragm." Said, "There's nothing that can be done for him." Said, "He can't live but a little while longer." Said, "We've sold our goods to follow. And we've done everything that we know how to do to get in the line. We've got cards, but never was called." And said, "Our money's gone and everything." Said, "We just--just couldn't do nothing." And said, "I prayed all night." And said, "This morning, I was kneeling on my knees by the side of the bed at the hotel, and I went to sleep." And said, "I dreamed that I should come over here to Miller's Cafeteria at nine o'clock." I looked at my watch, it was just exactly… [Blank.spot.on.tape.]…?…
text_no:
Jeg følte Herrens Engel... Du har sett bildet. Den bare kom ned. Du gjør bare det Den sier. Og jeg trodde vi skulle gå rett til Hobb's House, vi var omtrent en kvartal unna. Og Herrens Ånd sa: "Sving til venstre."
Jeg begynte å gå videre, og de andre fulgte etter. Da jeg stoppet ved et sted som het Miller's Cafeteria, gikk jeg ned i kjelleren hvor kafeteriaen var. Jeg tok noen svisker og litt ristet brød og satte meg ned for å spise. Og så hørte jeg noen si: "Pris Herren."
Jeg så bort, og en kvinne reiste seg med tårer rennende nedover kinnene. Fru Morgan, sykepleieren, sa: "Ser du? Nå er du fanget." 
Jeg svarte: "Vent litt."
Kvinnen kom bort og sa: "Bror Branham, jeg har fulgt deg fra møte til møte. Jeg har en bror her. Hjertet hans er ødelagt gjennom mellomgulvet. Legene kan ikke gjøre noe mer for ham. Han har bare kort tid igjen å leve." Hun fortsatte: "Vi har solgt alt vi eier for å følge etter deg. Vi har gjort alt vi kan for å komme i bønnekøen. Vi har kort, men ble aldri kalt opp. Nå er pengene våre borte. Jeg ba hele natten. I morges, mens jeg knelte ved siden av sengen på hotellet og sovnet, drømte jeg at jeg skulle komme hit til Miller's Cafeteria klokken ni."
Jeg så på klokken. Den var akkurat… [Tomt.område.på.lydbåndet.]…

text:
Do you believe in being led? That's the Holy Spirit that led Simeon. This is inside life, now. And I said, "Bring your brother here."
Why, in a moment, the Lord had healed him. He was crying and weeping. Said, "I never felt like this since I was a young boy."
Went out of the building, I set down again to eat, and Something just lifted me up. I went out, wife and them followed me. Just as I stepped out the door, I heard someone saying, "Oh, thank God." And a little woman dressed in black. She knelt down on the street. She begin to crying. And I said, "Stand up, sister."
And she stood up. She said, "Brother Branham." She was from Chicago. She said, "I've got cancer in the breast." She said, "I've tried hard to get to you, everywhere." Said, "I couldn't do it." And she said, "This morning, I started to eat. And I was crying, 'cause I have to go back home." And said, "The Spirit of the Lord said, 'Go stand in front of Miller's Cafeteria at ten minutes after nine.'"
text_no:
Tror du på å bli ledet? Det var Den Hellige Ånd som ledet Simeon. Dette er et indre liv. Jeg sa: "Ta med din bror hit."
I løpet av et øyeblikk hadde Herren helbredet ham. Han gråt og sa: "Jeg har aldri følt meg slik siden jeg var en ung gutt."
Jeg forlot bygningen, satte meg igjen for å spise, og noe løftet meg opp. Jeg gikk ut, og min kone fulgte etter. Idet jeg steg ut døren, hørte jeg noen si: "Å takk Gud." Det var en liten kvinne kledd i svart. Hun knelte ned på gaten og begynte å gråte. Jeg sa: "Reis Deg, søster."
Hun reiste seg og sa: "Bror Branham." Hun var fra Chicago. Hun sa: "Jeg har kreft i brystet." Hun fortalte at hun hadde prøvd hardt å komme til meg overalt, men hadde ikke klart det. Hun sa: "I morges begynte jeg å spise, og jeg gråt fordi jeg måtte reise hjem igjen." Så sa hun: "Den Hellige Ånd sa: 'Stå foran Miller's Cafeteria klokken ti minutter over ni.'"

text:
There it was. I met her over here in Arkansas the other day, just shouting. It's been weeks ago. Said, "The all… Everything had disappeared. It had gone."
And I started on down the street. I started… The wife said, "Aren't you going to eat?"
I said, "No. The Spirit of the Lord is leading."
And I went on across the street. And I started to go across to the drug store to get some little coloring books. We had to keep the baby locked up, a little three year old girl, in the room all day with us. And so I spend my time in prayer. That's how I know those things, is pray and pray.
And as I started across the street, Something said, "Stop."
I said, "Oh, my." I turned around.
Wife said, "Where you going?"
I said, "You all go right on to hotel. He's got something else for me to do." And I backed up in the corner to look at some fishing reels. I love to fish and so forth. I backed up there, see no one's looking at me, and I turned my head, and I said, "Father, what would You have me do?"
And stood there. Not just imagine now. I heard a Voice said, "Go to the corner."
text_no:
Der var det. Jeg møtte henne her i Arkansas for noen dager siden, bare ropende. Det har vært uker siden. Hun sa, "Alt... Alt hadde forsvunnet. Det var borte."
Jeg begynte å gå nedover gaten. Kona sa, "Skal du ikke spise?"
Jeg svarte, "Nei. Herrens Ånd leder meg."
Jeg krysset gaten og skulle over til apoteket for å kjøpe noen fargebøker. Vi måtte holde babyen, en liten tre år gammel jente, låst inne på rommet med oss hele dagen. Så jeg brukte min tid i bønn. Det er slik jeg vet disse tingene, ved å be og be.
Mens jeg gikk over gaten, sa Noe, "Stopp."
Jeg sa, "Åh, nei." Jeg snudde meg rundt.
Kona spurte, "Hvor skal du?"
Jeg svarte, "Dere kan gå tilbake til hotellet. Han har noe annet for meg å gjøre." Jeg trakk meg tilbake i hjørnet for å se på noen fiskesneller. Jeg elsker å fiske. Jeg sto der, passet på at ingen så på meg, og snudde hodet. Så sa jeg, "Far, hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre?"
Jeg sto der. Ikke bare forestille deg nå. Jeg hørte en Stemme si, "Gå til hjørnet."

text:
I went down to the corner, across the street. And I stood there, and they begin to blow the whistle, and the traffic would come across the street, and--and they walked there with the lights. And--and I stood there for about ten minutes. And just kept standing there. After while, the whistle blowed again, the group of people crossed the street, just as busy as they could be.
And I noticed coming behind was a lady with a little checkered dress on. She had on a little Canadian…?… And she's carrying a pocket book over her arm. She walked across the street, and the Spirit of the Lord said, "Go near to her."
And I walked right up to her like that, and she passed by looking down, passed on by me. Now, I thought, "That's strange. Maybe God just did something there that I didn't know."
text_no:
Jeg gikk ned til hjørnet, tvers over gaten. Jeg sto der og ventet mens fløyta blåste og trafikken passerte gaten, ledet av lysene. Jeg sto der i omtrent ti minutter og bare observerte. Etter hvert blåste fløyta igjen, og en gruppe mennesker krysset gaten, fulle av aktivitet.
Jeg la merke til en dame som kom bak gruppen. Hun hadde på seg en rutete kjole og en liten kanadisk jakke. Hun bar en lommebok over armen. Da hun gikk over gaten, sa Herrens Ånd: "Gå nær henne."
Jeg gikk rett bort til henne, men hun passerte forbi meg mens hun så ned. Jeg tenkte: "Det er merkelig. Kanskje Gud gjorde noe der som jeg ikke var klar over."

text:
She walked about fifteen feet. She turned back and looked like that. She said, "Oh, Brother Branham." And she said--she said, "Oh…" She just begin to beat herself. Said… Oh, she didn't knowed what's happened.
I said, "What's the matter, sister?"
She said, "I'm from Canada." She said, "I've spent every penny that I've had. I'm only allowed a hundred and fifty dollars." She said, "I've got a withered hand." And she said, "I slept in a lobby last night, and had five cents for coffee this morning." And she said, "I was going out here on the road to hitch-hike to get back home." A young woman, about thirty years old. She said, "And I was going down here about two squares, and Something said to me, 'Turn this a way.'"
Oh, my. There it was. I said, "Stretch me your hand, sister."
And there it come her hand. And that big Irish cop standing out there looked. He said, "I know you, Brother Branham."
And here he come running in. My, group of people there. They had to get a gang to get away. Everybody around, a healing service. The Lord working.
text_no:
Hun gikk omtrent fem meter. Hun snudde seg og sa, "Å, Bror Branham." Så begynte hun å slå seg selv og sa, "Å…" Hun visste ikke hva som hadde skjedd.
Jeg spurte, "Hva er det, søster?"
Hun svarte, "Jeg er fra Canada. Jeg har brukt opp alle pengene mine. Jeg har bare lov til å ha med meg hundre og femti dollar. Jeg har en visnet hånd. Jeg sov i en lobby i natt og hadde fem cent til kaffe i morges. Jeg skulle gå ut på veien for å haike hjem." Hun var en ung kvinne på omtrent tretti år. "Jeg gikk nedover gaten her, to kvartaler unna, og noe sa til meg, 'Snur denne veien.'"
Å, du store. Der var det. Jeg sa, "Rekk ut hånden, søster."
Og der kom hånden hennes. Den store irske politimannen som stod der så det. Han sa, "Jeg kjenner deg, Bror Branham."
Og så kom han løpende inn. For en mengde mennesker! De måtte få folk til å rydde veien. Alle rundt, en helbredelsestjeneste. Herren virket.

text:
God leads. Don't you believe that? That's right. Here not long ago, coming down out of a meeting, I was going out. This happened in--in this, at the Camden, Arkansas. I was going out of the meeting. And just see how the Lord deals.
First, just before I took off my uniform, I was a Indiana State game warden three years ago. And at… Just before I took off my uniform, the Lord had called me, and has appeared to me, and the Angel of the Lord, as I've told you how it happened.
text_no:
Gud leder. Tror du ikke det? Det er riktig. For ikke lenge siden, da jeg kom ned fra et møte, var jeg på vei ut. Dette skjedde i Camden, Arkansas. Jeg var på vei ut av møtet. Bare se hvordan Herren handler.
Først, like før jeg tok av meg uniformen, var jeg viltvokter i Indiana for tre år siden. Og rett før jeg tok av meg uniformen, hadde Herren kalt meg og åpenbart Seg for meg. Herrens Engel, som jeg har fortalt dere hvordan det skjedde.

text:
And I went down, my little girl was just born. I went down to get her some of them bottle caps for her catnip tea, or whatever it was. And I had my twenty-eight dollar check I was going to cash. And I went into… And the bus stopped on Spring Street there, at the little city I lived. I noticed the strange acting man got off, and he looked at me. I went into the drug store and cashed my check, and--and got the bottle caps, and come back. And as I started out the street, someone laid their hand on me. And I turned around. He said, "Sir, you an officer I see?"
And I said, "Yes sir. I work for the Indiana Conservation."
He said, "Seeing that you was an officer, I want to ask you a question."
I said, "Yes sir."
And he said, "I'd better tell you first." He said, "I--I been failing in health for about two years."
text_no:
Jeg dro nedover byen etter at datteren min nettopp var født. Jeg skulle hente noen flaskehetter til hennes kattemynte-te eller hva det enn var. Jeg hadde med meg en sjekk på tjueåtte dollar som jeg skulle heve. Bussen stoppet på Spring Street i den lille byen jeg bodde i. Jeg la merke til en mann som oppførte seg merkelig, og han så på meg. Jeg gikk inn på apoteket, hevet sjekken og kjøpte flaskehettene. Da jeg kom tilbake ut på gaten, var det noen som la hånden sin på meg. Jeg snudde meg rundt. Han sa: "Er du en offiser?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja, jeg jobber for Indiana Conservation."
Han sa: "Når jeg ser at du er en offiser, vil jeg stille deg et spørsmål."
Jeg svarte: "Ja, det kan du."
Han sa: "Jeg bør kanskje fortelle deg først. Jeg har hatt dårlig helse de siste to årene."

text:
And the Angel had just appeared to me about four or five days before that and told me about this. And he said… or about the gift. And he said, "I was… I live at Paducah, Kentucky." He said, "Last night, I had a strange dream." He said, "I seen an Angel coming down out of heaven. And he came down and told me to come to this city of Jeffersonville, and to inquire for somebody by the name of Branham to pray for me." Said, "Would you know where there was any such person here, anybody by the name of Branham?"
Oh, my, my heart just felt like it was that big. And I said, "My mother runs a boarding house right around the corner."
He said, "You're…"
I said, "My mother."
He said, "Is your name Branham?"
I had to give him my arms. I said, "Brother, come here to the corner." And I begin to tell him what happened. And he started weeping. And we knelt there on the street, right there on the street, and I prayed for him. And when I got up, the people holding their children back and taking off their hats in respect there on the street. And God moved down from the heavens and healed him there.
text_no:
Engelen hadde nettopp vist seg for meg rundt fire eller fem dager før dette og fortalte meg om denne hendelsen, spesifikt om gaven. Han sa, "Jeg bor i Paducah, Kentucky. I går natt hadde jeg en merkelig drøm. Jeg så en Engel komme ned fra himmelen. Han kom og fortalte meg at jeg skulle reise til denne byen Jeffersonville og spørre etter en person ved navn Branham for å be for meg." Han spurte: "Vet du om en slik person her, noen ved navn Branham?"
Å, hjertet mitt føltes som om det var kjempestort. Jeg sa, "Min mor driver et pensjonat rett rundt hjørnet."
Han spurte, "Er du..."
Jeg sa, "Min mor."
Han spurte, "Er ditt navn Branham?"
Jeg strakte ut armene mine og sa, "Bror, kom hit til hjørnet." Jeg begynte å fortelle ham hva som hadde skjedd, og han begynte å gråte. Vi knelte der i gata, og jeg ba for ham. Da vi reiste oss, så jeg at folk holdt barna sine tilbake og tok av seg hattene i respekt. Og Gud kom ned fra himmelen og helbredet ham der.

text:
And a few weeks later, I was coming out of a building, and I heard somebody hollering, "Mercy, mercy!"
Four cops was bringing me out, getting me through the crowd. There was thousands pressing in. And the night before that, the Angel of the Lord came right into the building where I was, come down. And there was people there was lame, halt, blind, and everything. I was trying to explain, getting the people to believe it. I said, "Can't you believe?" I said, "I've told you the truth." And I said, "It's the truth."
My brother walking by calling time on me. I know I talk too much. That's right. But I--I love you people, and I--I want to… I want to tell you this anyhow.
And there was a… As I came there to the--the place, I was talking, telling them about the Spirit of the Lord. And just about that time, coming in the front door, here It came. I said, "I won't have to speak any more. Here It is Itself."
text_no:
Noen uker senere kom jeg ut av en bygning og hørte noen rope: "Nåde, nåde!" 
Fire politimenn eskorterte meg gjennom folkemengden. Det var tusenvis som presset på. Kvelden før hadde Herrens Engel kommet inn i bygningen der jeg var. Det var mange til stede som var lamme, halte og blinde. Jeg forsøkte å forklare og få folk til å tro. Jeg sa: "Kan dere ikke tro?" Jeg sa: "Jeg har fortalt dere sannheten," og jeg sa: "Det er sannheten."
Min bror gikk forbi og minnet meg på tiden. Jeg vet at jeg snakker for mye, men jeg elsker dere, og jeg vil fortelle dere dette likevel.
Da jeg nærmet meg stedet, snakket jeg om Herrens Ånd. Akkurat da, når jeg kom inn gjennom hoveddøren, kom Den. Jeg sa: "Jeg trenger ikke å si mer. Her Er Det selv."

text:
And It came right up through the building like that. Circled around where I was, like that. And people blind, and deaf, and dumb, and everything, just being--raising up and walking through the building glorifying God.
The next day, I was being taken from a--a church where I was speaking by four officers. I started out of the building, and I heard somebody hollering, "Mercy, mercy, mercy!"
And I looked over, standing over out of the crowd was an old blind colored man, little white rim of hair around his head with a cap, crying, "Mercy, mercy!"
He couldn't stand with the white people, of course. And he was crying like that. And I started, and Something moved, said, "Go over there."
And I said, "Let's go over to where that man is."
And he said… the officer said, "Oh, Brother Branham, you couldn't put your hand on that man." Said, "They'd arrest you." Said, "You can't do that; you're in Arkansas."
I said, "Well, look. But the Holy Spirit tells me to go over there…"
Said, "You'd start a race riot." Said, "You can't do that. This is the South."
And I said, "I can't help where it's at. The Lord tells me go over here, and I…"
text_no:
Den kom rett opp gjennom bygningen. Den sirklet rundt der jeg var. Folk som var blinde, døve, stumme og alt mulig annet, reiste seg og gikk gjennom bygningen mens de æret Gud.
Neste dag skulle jeg eskorteres fra en menighet der jeg hadde talt av fire politimenn. Da vi var på vei ut av bygningen, hørte jeg noen rope: "Miskunn, miskunn, miskunn!"
Jeg så over mot folkemengden og der sto en gammel blind mann med mørk hud, med en liten hvit hårkant rundt hodet og en lue, som ropte: "Miskunn, miskunn!"
Han kunne selvfølgelig ikke stå sammen med de hvite. Han ropte slik, og jeg begynte å bevege meg mot ham da noe virket i meg og sa: "Gå over dit."
Jeg sa: "La oss gå bort til den mannen."
Offiseren svarte: "Å, Bror Branham, du kan ikke legge hånden på den mannen. De ville arrestere deg. Du kan ikke gjøre det; du er i Arkansas."
Jeg sa: "Men Den Hellige Ånd sier at jeg skal gå dit."
Han sa: "Du vil starte et raseopptøye. Du kan ikke gjøre det. Dette er Sørstatene."
Jeg svarte: "Jeg kan ikke gjøre noe med hvor vi er. Herren sier at jeg skal gå dit, og jeg…"

text:
They went with me. And they drawed a ring around. And I never will forget. I heard his wife say, "Honey, the parson is coming towards you--the parson."
And so I left, and I come up to where he was. And he said, "Is--is--is--is--is this you, Parson Branham?" Said, "Can I feeled your face?" And he put his old quivering hands over my face.
I said, "Yes, sir."
He said, "Parson Branham, can you listen just a moment to my story?"
I said, "Yes, sir. Go right ahead."
And the people just trying to break through, but they were holding their arms, holding the people back. And he said, "Parson Branham," Said, "my old mammy had 'ligion like you got." "'Ligion like you got." Said, "Her never told me lie all her life." Said, "She's been dead now for ten years." Said, "I's been blind for about eight years." And said, "Last night," said, "I never heard of you in my life." And said, "Last night, about eight o'clock." Said, "I live about a hundred and fifty miles from here." Said, "My old mammy stood right by the side of my bed. And she said, 'Honey child, you go down to Camden, Arkansas and ask for someone by the name of Parson Branham, and you'll get your sight.'"
Oh, my. I just put my hand upon his poor old dark wrinkled face. I said, "Lord Jesus, I do not understand this. But I ask for his sight in Thy Name."
